Off Calendar Sep-18-2025 Continued To May-04-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two
Off Calendar Apr-09-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF CLARISSA MARQUEZ's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Denied as untimely per CCP 2031.310(c). American Honda Motor Co. served verified responses via email on January 13, 2025. (3/25/25 Doudar decl. Ex. C.) Marquez's deadline to move to compel further responses ran on March 3, 2025, 45 days from January 13, 2025, plus two days to account for e-service. (CCP 2031.310(c); CCP 1010.6.) Marquez filed this motion on March 11, 2025, over a week past deadline. The 45-day limit is mandatory and the court is "without authority" to compel further responses. (See Sexton v.Superior Court (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 1403, 1410.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar Dec-12-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (Via Zoom videoconference).
Atty. ROY ENAV, Esq. of QUILL & ARROW, LLP (Tel: (310) 933-4271) for plaintiff.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing re: PLAINTIFF CLARISSA MARQUEZ's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One.

Matter not argued/contested. The court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Clarissa A. Marquez's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents, Set One is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Defendant American Honda Motor Company, Inc. is ordered to produce all documents responsive to Request #30 of Set One within 21 days of service of notice of this order. The request is not vague or unduly overbroad. Information regarding similar complaints, repairs, etc. is discoverable under the broad standard of CCP 2031.010. Whether Defendant has received similar complaints regarding similar cars or conducted-or been unable to conduct-repairs, for example, may well be relevant and admissible. (See Donlen v. Ford Motor Co., supra, (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 138, 143144, 153; Doppes v. Bentley Motors, Inc. (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 967, 973, 978-979, 986); thus, Plaintiff's requests for such information are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence and Defendant's contentions and arguments do not establish otherwise. Defendant's refusal to produce any documents responsive to Request #30 is unreasonable.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. =(301/JMQ)
